2604.17085.title: Comparing Human and Large Language Model Interpretation of Implicit Information
2604.17085.paper_summary: The interpretation of implicit meanings is an integral aspect of human communication. However, this framework may not transfer to interactions with Large Language Models (LLMs). To investigate this, we introduce the task of Implicit Information Extraction (IIE) and propose an LLM-based IIE pipeline that builds a structured knowledge graph from a context sentence by extracting relational triplets, validating implicit inferences, and analyzing temporal relations. We evaluate two LLMs against crowdsourced human judgments on two datasets. We find that humans agree with most model triplets yet consistently propose many additions, indicating limited coverage in current LLM-based IIE. Moreover, in our experiments, models appear to be more conservative about implicit inferences than humans in socially rich contexts, whereas humans become more conservative in shorter, fact-oriented contexts. Our code is available at https://github.com/Antonio-Dee/IIE_from_LLM.
2604.17085.paper_summary_zh: 隱含意義的詮釋是人類溝通的一個重要方面。<lb/>然而，這一框架可能無法轉移到與大型語言模型（LLMs）的互動中。<lb/>為了調查這一點，我們引入了隱含信息提取（IIE）的任務，並提出了一個基於LLM的IIE流程，該流程通過提取關係三元組、驗證隱含推論和分析時間關係，從上下文句子構建結構化知識圖譜。<lb/>我們在兩個數據集上對兩個LLM進行了評估，並與來自群眾的人工判斷進行比較。<lb/>我們發現人類對大多數模型三元組達成一致，但卻持續提出許多補充，這表明當前基於LLM的IIE的覆蓋範圍有限。<lb/>此外，在我們的實驗中，模型在社會豐富的上下文中對隱含推論似乎比人類更為保守，而人類在較短、以事實為導向的上下文中則變得更為保守。<lb/>我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/Antonio-Dee/IIE_from_LLM 獲得。
2604.17085.author: Antonio De Santis et.al.
2604.17085.authors: Antonio De Santis, Tommaso Bonetti, Andrea Tocchetti, Marco Brambilla
